applicability_notes: Elementwise creation op mirroring input tensor shape/dtype. Commonly used to preallocate output buffers for ops with out= parameter (cumsum, cumprod, sort) and as the zero-initialized base tensor for ttnn.scatter in MoE routing (scatter into dense routing table) and multimodal embedding merge. dtype can be overridden (e.g. ttnn.uint16 for sort indices). Fusable into downstream scatter/copy chains as the zero-fill source.

placement_observations.size_descriptor: same shape as input, e.g. [1,1,S,E] router probs or [B*S,H] flattened embeddings
placement_observations.source: test_examples: ttnn.zeros_like(input_tensor) with no explicit memory_config inherits default interleaved DRAM; ttnn.is_tensor_storage_on_device(output_tensor) asserted true

placement_observations.size_descriptor: same shape as input, TILE layout
placement_observations.source: test_examples: preallocated_output_tensor = ttnn.zeros_like(input_tensor, dtype=ttnn_dtype, layout=ttnn.Layout.TILE) then passed as out= to ttnn.cumsum/cumprod/sort

placement_observations.size_descriptor: [1,1,S,num_experts] dense routing tensor or [B*S,H] flattened input embeddings
placement_observations.source: call_sites: ttnn.scatter(ttnn.zeros_like(router_probs), dim=-1, ...) in MoE router; zeros = ttnn.zeros_like(input_embeds) reused for deepstack visual embed scatter
